Ad Text:
We are seeking an experienced Learning & Development professional to design, deliver, and continuously improve high-impact learning solutions that strengthen capability, accelerate performance, and deliver measurable impact across the business. 

This role sits within the APAC L&D team and partners closely with business leaders, HR, and global L&D colleagues. It combines program ownership, facilitation, content development, and internal consulting across both early careers and experienced populations, ensuring learning solutions are aligned to business priorities and drive measurable outcomes.  

We operate in a fast-paced, high-performance environment where learning is expected to be practical, relevant, and directly linked to business outcomes. This role requires someone who can engage a highly analytical audience and deliver solutions that drive real performance improvement. 

What You Will Do 

Own and evolve early careers programs 

Lead the design, delivery, and continuous improvement of intern and graduate programs 

Partner with campus recruiting, trader education, and business stakeholders to align programs to capability and performance needs 

Ensure programs are structured, scalable, and deliver a strong participant experience 

Design and deliver professional development solutions 

Build learning solutions that strengthen professional, technical, and leadership capability  

Create structured development journeys for employees at different career stages 

Contribute to firm-wide capability building initiatives 

Facilitate high-impact learning experiences 

Deliver engaging, practical learning experiences (in-person and virtual) across APAC, embedding Susquehanna’s culture and ways of working 

Adapt facilitation approach to engage audiences from early career talent to experienced professionals across technical and business functions, including technology, operations, trading, and quantitative research 

Tailor delivery style to suit a fast-paced, high-performance environment 

Partner with the business as an L&D consultant 

Diagnose capability gaps and performance challenges with managers and senior leaders 

Translate business priorities into targeted, outcome-driven learning solutions 

Provide credible challenge and guidance to shape effective development approaches 

Build scalable learning solutions 

Design and develop eLearning, toolkits, and on-demand resources 

Partner with subject matter experts to create practical, relevant content 

Curate and integrate internal and external learning resources 

Support and Contribute to L&D operations and global alignment 

Support the implementation and management of learning platforms in the region 

Partner with global L&D to adapt and deliver initiatives locally 

Ensure alignment with global frameworks while addressing regional requirements 

8–10 years of experience in Learning & Development, with a track record of owning and delivering programs end-to-end 

Demonstrated ability to facilitate engaging learning experiences across in-person and virtual environments 

Experience designing early careers programs and professional development solutions 

Strong consulting and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence managers and senior leaders 

Ability to translate business needs into practical, outcome-driven learning solutions, using curiosity to uncover root needs and challenge assumptions 

Experience designing scalable learning solutions, including eLearning and digital resources 

Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage competing priorities and collaborate across teams to deliver 

Experience in the creation of eLearning modules highly preferred 

Excellent communication skills, with the ability to engage and challenge stakeholders 

Proven ability to operate independently and take ownership in a fast-paced, high-performance environment 

What’s in it for you 

Our flat hierarchy enables you to drive meaningful impact, by leveraging your expertise and taking ownership.
Private healthcare and gym allowances, plus wellness initiatives.
Additional leave entitlements including marriage leave and 2 bonus days annually. 
Fully stocked kitchen, with daily breakfast and lunch.
Regular social, sporting and community events including annual poker tournament.
Matched donations and corporate discounts.

Explore our benefits and culture: link

About Susquehanna

Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together.

What we do 

Susquehanna’s deep integration of trading, technology and quant research makes us experts in trading essentially all listed financial products and asset classes, with a focus on derivatives. We handle millions of trading transactions around the world every day as both a market maker and market taker. Our efforts provide liquidity and ensure competitive prices for buyers and sellers. While our presence in the market is broad, our trading desks are highly specialized to allow for a deep understanding of the unique drivers of each asset class.

Equal Opportunity Statement

We encourage applications from candidates from all backgrounds, and we welcome requests for reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process to ensure that you can best demonstrate your abilities.

#LI-RV1 LI-Onsite

Job Title: Principal Product Manager, Rovo Search and AI Quality
Ad Text:
Working at Atlassian

Atlassians can choose where they work – whether in an office, from home, or a combination of the two. That way, Atlassians have more control over supporting their family, personal goals, and other priorities. We can hire people in any country where we have a legal entity.

The Rovo + AI team’s vision is to accelerate AI innovation across Atlassian by building the core infrastructure, key capabilities and experiences. The Rovo + AI team is building some core AI products, in enterprise Search and conversational AI space that leverage Atlassian’s key strength - the understanding of our customers' work and organizational knowledge. We are in still early stages of building these experiences. We expect a lot of exploration, experimentation, validation of hypotheses through customer research to figure out the right solutions.

Strategic Vision And Execution

As a Principal Product Manager for Search and AI Quality in Central AI, you will play a pivotal role in shaping and executing Atlassian’s vision for delivering quality AI products - especially focusing on Rovo Search. Your focus will be on delivering industry leading enterprise search that underpin Atlassian’s AI-driven products and solutions. Key responsibilities include:

Define and drive the strategic vision and roadmap for AI quality for Search/Search Agents, clearly articulating the desired outcomes and the path to achieve them.

Lead initiatives to improve the quality, reliability, and trustworthiness of AI models and experiences, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and user expectations.

Cross-functional Collaboration

Partner with engineering, data science, product, and design teams to identify opportunities and implement improvements in AI quality.

Advocate for relevance and quality standards, influencing stakeholders and championing initiatives that elevate user experience.

Customer-Centric Approach

Develop deep customer insights to pinpoint gaps in quality, proactively addressing issues to enhance user satisfaction and engagement.

Regularly interact with users and internal stakeholders to validate quality enhancements and prioritize feature improvements.

Communication And Storytelling

Create compelling narratives and communicate progress effectively to customers, stakeholders, and the broader community, highlighting Atlassian’s advancements in search relevance and quality.

To succeed in this role, you should bring a robust combination of technical depth, strategic thinking, and hands-on expertise in building AI quality focused products. Key qualifications include:

8+ years of product management experience, with substantial recent experience driving relevance and quality for a consumer or enterprise AI product.

Proven proficiency in defining and improving on relevance and quality measurement metrics.

Strong technical subject matter expertise, with a comprehensive understanding of search/ads technologies, natural language processing, and machine learning principles.

Data-driven decision-making skills, including the ability to independently analyze complex data sets, identify trends, and demonstrate the customer impact of improvements.

Deep user empathy and commitment to delivering exceptional experiences, with attention to detail that drives continuous improvement.

Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills, capable of conveying complex technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences, including senior executives.

Demonstrated ability to influence and drive cross-functional initiatives without direct authority, maintaining alignment across diverse teams.

Bachelor's or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field preferred.

Benefits & Perks

Atlassian offers a wide range of perks and benefits designed to support you, your family and to help you engage with your local community. Our offerings include health and wellbeing resources, paid volunteer days, and so much more. To learn more, visit go.atlassian.com/perksandbenefits.

About Atlassian

At Atlassian, we're motivated by a common goal: to unleash the potential of every team. Our software products help teams all over the planet and our solutions are designed for all types of work. Team collaboration through our tools makes what may be impossible alone, possible together.

We believe that the unique contributions of all Atlassians create our success. To ensure that our products and culture continue to incorporate everyone's perspectives and experience, we never discriminate based on race, religion, national origin, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, or marital, veteran, or disability status. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

To provide you the best experience, we can support with accommodations or adjustments at any stage of the recruitment process. Simply inform our Recruitment team during your conversation with them.

To learn more about our culture and hiring process, visit go.atlassian.com/crh.

Who We Are

At Acusensus, we don’t just build technology; we change behaviours and save lives. We are an Australian technology company with operations across Australia, the United Kingdom, the United States, and New Zealand. Founded in 2018, Acusensus has built world-leading AI and sensing technology for road safety.

We’re now looking for a commercially minded AI Adoption Specialist to accelerate the integration of Artificial Intelligence across our global operations and embed AI as a practical, value‑creating capability across the business.

Why You’ll Love Working With Us
Mission with Meaning: Your work will directly support technology that saves lives and prevents injuries, applying AI where it delivers measurable real‑world impact.
Enterprise‑Wide Influence: This is not a lab-based or theoretical role, you’ll work side‑by‑side with leaders and teams to transform the way Acusensus operates through smart, practical AI adoption.
Innovation‑Driven Culture: Join a high‑performing, collaborative organisation that encourages curiosity, continuous improvement, and bold thinking while balancing innovation with responsibility.
The Opportunity

As the AI Adoption Specialist, you will play a pivotal role in advancing AI maturity across Acusensus. Acting as a bridge between strategy, operations, and technology, you will identify, prioritise, and lead high‑impact AI initiatives that demonstrably improve business performance, decision‑making, and operational efficiency.

Reporting to the Head of Product and Service Operations, this role is based in Melbourne or Sydney and works closely with senior leaders, delivery teams, and internal AI champions across the global business.

You will define and maintain a clear AI adoption roadmap, ensure initiatives are backed by strong cost–benefit analysis, and embed change through effective engagement, coaching, and change management.

What You’ll Do
Identify High‑Impact Opportunities: Evaluate business processes and workflows to pinpoint where AI can deliver the greatest strategic, operational, and financial benefit.
Link AI to Business Outcomes: Ensure every AI initiative is tied directly to measurable performance improvements and commercial value.
Lead AI Initiatives End‑to‑End: Take a hands‑on role in rolling out major AI‑driven initiatives, from concept through to full business adoption.
Build AI Capability: Develop and coach a network of internal “AI Champions” to drive localised improvements and sustained adoption.
Shape the AI Roadmap: Own and evolve Acusensus’ internal AI adoption roadmap, balancing innovation, risk, and return on investment.
Report and Advise: Provide clear reporting and insights to senior leaders on AI adoption progress, opportunities, risks, and emerging trends.
What We’re Looking For
Strong Technical & Business Foundation: Tertiary qualification in Information Technology, Data Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field.
Proven AI or Digital Transformation Experience: Demonstrated experience implementing AI‑driven solutions or leading digital transformation initiatives in complex environments.
Commercial & Strategic Mindset: Ability to connect AI capabilities to tangible business outcomes using sound prioritisation and cost–benefit analysis.
Influential Communicator: Exceptional relationship‑building and influencing skills, with the confidence to drive change across all levels of the organisation.
Adaptable & Curious: A fast learner who thrives in rapidly evolving technology landscapes and enjoys exploring what’s possible.
What We Offer
Competitive salary package
Flexible working arrangements
National travel opportunities with global exposure
The chance to work with cutting‑edge AI and imaging technology
A collaborative, purpose‑driven global team
A culture built on Impact, Collaboration, Integrity, Care, and Delivery
How to Apply

If you’re passionate about applying AI in ways that truly matter and want to help shape the future of a fast‑growing global technology company, we’d love to hear from you. For confidential enquiries, please contact careers@acusensus.com.

Acusensus is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to make a meaningful impact

Lead our Business Performance (VCFO) offering and drive high-impact advisory outcomes across Australia and New Zealand

Following an internal promotion, we are seeking a Business Performance Lead (VCFO) to drive our business performance offering. You will lead the daily operations of the Business Performance team to ensure our advisory services are scalable, standardised, and commercially sustainable. Your focus will be on delivering high-impact strategic and financial planning for senior, high-trust client engagements. 

This opportunity is open to candidates across Australia, with the ability to work from any of our regional or metropolitan hubs.

Your role, your contribution

Client Delivery Leadership: Oversee the delivery of the Business Performance (VCFO) offering ensuring consistent quality and scope discipline. You will personally lead Strategic Planning workshops and senior advisory engagements, including board reporting and growth initiatives.

Operational Management: Own the weekly operating cadence including work allocation, WIP control, and quality checks. You will manage the pipeline and resolve resourcing constraints to ensure timely delivery.

Commercial Ownership: Manage a profitable portfolio by overseeing engagement economics, margins, and fee estimations. You will play a key role in proposal development and shaping solutions for new clients.

People Leadership: Provide direct coaching and mentoring to the Business Performance team. You will focus on uplifting consulting capabilities and developing Senior Client Managers into senior advisory practitioners.

Standardisation and Improvement: Drive the use of standard templates and methods to ensure delivery is repeatable and not dependent on individual preference. You will partner with the Head of Products and Services to implement automation and tools.

Stakeholder Integration: Build strong relationships with Partners and Client Teams to ensure seamless handoffs and clear communication throughout the sales and delivery process.

The benefits of joining us

Flexible working arrangements through our flexible working policy.

Option to purchase additional leave to support life commitments and planned breaks.

Structured leadership programs and development pathways, with coaching and learning support.

Strong internal networks and support across Findex to help you succeed in the role.

What you bring to the role

You are an experienced advisory professional from a public practice background who enjoys both high-level strategy and the operational mechanics of leading a team. You possess:

8 or more years of experience in professional services (accounting, advisory, or consulting) with significant SME advisory exposure.

Proven expertise in designing and facilitating advisory workshops and delivering strategic outputs.

A strong technical grounding in management reporting, three-way forecasting, and scenario modelling.

Demonstrated leadership skills with a focus on coaching and uplifting the consulting capabilities of others.

The ability to influence stakeholders and partners within a matrix structure.

A Bachelor of Business or an equivalent tertiary qualification.

Our Impact: Why Findex?

At Findex, we help clients manage, grow, and protect their financial future with confidence. Since 1987, we’ve become a leading Australasian provider of integrated accounting, advisory, and wealth solutions, delivered through our “Wealth Accountant” and Family Office models.

For the Community: The Findex Community Fund (FCF) is dedicated to giving back. Since 2018, we have donated over $4.3 million to more than 800 charities across Australia and New Zealand.

For You: We focus on developing our people first. With structured training and development opportunities, we allow our people to grow their career in a way that sets them up for success.

For our Customers: We champion the success of over 75,000 Australasian businesses, helping them grow with expert advice, fresh thinking, and top-tier professional services.

For Diversity & Inclusion: Findex is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

If this role sounds like a good fit: Apply now.

Senior Clinical Project Manager

Locations: Australia & New Zealand

The Role

At IQVIA, we accelerate innovation to improve patient outcomes worldwide. As a Senior Clinical Project Manager, you will independently lead and deliver medium to large, multi‑regional clinical studies, partnering closely with clinical teams and customers to bring new therapies to market faster. You will hold end‑to‑end responsibility for study delivery in line with contractual requirements and SOPs, acting as the primary customer contact and leading cross‑functional teams to achieve milestones on time, within budget and to the highest quality standards, while driving strong financial and customer outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Lead and contribute to bid defence activities in partnership with Business Development for multi‑service, multi‑country, and/or multi‑regional studies
Translate project strategy into clear, executable operational plans
Develop and maintain integrated study management plans with the core project team
Provide strategic leadership and oversight to ensure delivery of clinical studies in line with scope, timelines, quality, and cost expectations
Lead, motivate and manage cross‑functional project teams to achieve agreed objectives and milestones
Act as the primary point of contact for customers, owning and strengthening key client relationships
Monitor progress against contractual obligations and proactively communicate project status, risks, and solutions to internal and external stakeholders
Manage project risks, issues, and contingencies, leading problem‑solving and resolution efforts
Ensure project quality by identifying risks, responding to issues, and implementing corrective and preventive action plans
Drive financial success through forecasting, revenue acceleration opportunities and change control management
Identify changes in scope and manage the change control process effectively
Capture and share lessons learned and best practices to support continuous improvement
Support staff development through mentoring, coaching and performance feedback
Act as a change advocate, adopting and promoting corporate initiatives

Essential Qualifications & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Life Sciences or a related field
Minimum 10 years of clinical research experience, including at least 6 years in clinical project management
Minimum 5 years’ experience delivering healthy volunteer (Phase I) studies
Proven experience managing medium to large, multi‑regional, or full‑service clinical trials
Strong working knowledge of ICH‑GCP and applicable local regulatory requirements

Key Skills & Competencies

Strong understanding of clinical trial conduct, protocols, and therapeutic areas
Ability to lead through others, drive results, manage performance and work effectively across functions and geographies
Excellent written, verbal and presentation skills; strong negotiation and customer management capability
Proactive, analytical, and solutions‑focused approach
Demonstrated ability to manage competing priorities and complex timelines
Experience managing project financials, forecasts, and contractual obligations
High attention to detail, continuous improvement mindset and learning agility
Proficiency with MS Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
Strong relationship‑building skills with clients, peers, and stakeholders

Why IQVIA?

IQVIA is a global leader in clinical research, commercial insights, and healthcare intelligence. We offer:

Competitive salary package with 2 additional annual leave days per year
Flexibility to work at home and in 1 of 3 IQVIA offices (Hybrid for metro NSW, metro VIC, Auckland NZ) OR 100% remote if in QLD, WA, SA, TAS, regional NSW/VIC and NZ
Exposure to innovative and impactful clinical research
Opportunities to lead complex, global studies
A collaborative, high‑performance culture
Career development, flexibility and support across Australia and New Zealand

Own the study. Lead the science. Make a global impact

Apply today with you resume and a member of the Talent Acquisition team will be in contact via email

Referrals welcome

IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com

IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.

Job Profile Summary

The OCM Senior Analyst plays a key role in designing, leading, and delivering organisational change initiatives that enable leaders, teams, and organisations to successfully adopt changes across business processes, systems, technology, and operating models. Working as a trusted member of the Unisys Organisational Change Management (OCM) team, this role partners closely with OCM Managers, project leadership, and senior stakeholders to drive measurable adoption outcomes.

The Senior OCM Analyst brings strong hands‑on experience, sound judgment, and methodological discipline to change delivery—owning workstreams end‑to‑end, coaching junior team members, and ensuring change activities are aligned to business outcomes, risk management, and benefits realisation.

Key Responsibilities

Change Strategy & Delivery

Lead the design and execution of OCM activities aligned to project objectives, business outcomes, and stakeholder needs.

Conduct and interpret change impact, readiness, and risk assessments at individual, team, and organisational levels.

Develop and tailor integrated change strategies, including communications, engagement, training, and resistance management plans.

Proactively identify change risks and adoption barriers; design mitigation strategies to address resistance and capability gaps.

Track, analyse, and report on adoption metrics, behavioural indicators, and change effectiveness to inform decision‑making.

Contribute to sustainment planning to embed change into business‑as‑usual operations.

Translate complex transformation initiatives into clear, actionable messages for diverse audiences.

Communication & Engagement

Lead the development of high‑impact, audience‑specific communications (executive briefings, change narratives, FAQs, campaigns).

Create multimedia and digital collateral (videos, infographics, intranet content) to drive awareness and engagement.

Ensure messaging is consistent, timely, and aligned with overall program governance and brand standards.

Training & Learning Support

Lead training needs analysis and learning strategy development aligned to role‑based capability requirements.

Design and review training materials, learning journeys, and reinforcement mechanisms.

Support and co‑facilitate training sessions for frontline staff, leaders, and executives as required.

Ensure training effectiveness is measured and iterated based on feedback and adoption outcomes.

Collaboration & Project Support

Work autonomously while aligning closely with OCM Managers, Program Leads, and delivery partners.

Facilitate workshops, feedback sessions, and stakeholder briefings to gather insights and refine approaches.

Contribute to OCM practice development, standards, templates, and continuous improvement initiatives.

Support governance activities including status reporting, risk escalation, and executive updates.

Required Skills & Capabilities

Strong written, verbal, and visual communication skills in English; additional languages (e.g., German, Dutch, other European languages) are an advantage.

Proven ability to design and deliver end‑to‑end change management activities across complex initiatives.

Strong stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills, including experience working with senior leaders.

Ability to synthesise complex information and data into clear insights and practical recommendations.

Solid working knowledge of change management methodologies (e.g., Prosci / ADKAR, CMBoK) and their practical application.

Creative mindset with interest in using design tools (e.g., Adobe Creative Cloud or similar) for basic graphic or video editing.

Proficiency with Microsoft Office tools (PowerPoint, Word, Excel, SharePoint).

High level of organisation, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.

Proactive, adaptable, and comfortable operating in fast‑paced, matrixed environments.

Preferred / Desirable Skills

Experience supporting large‑scale digital transformation, system implementations, or operating model changes.

Background in Business Process Improvement, Knowledge Management, Learning & Development, or Instructional Design.

Familiarity with reporting and dashboard tools (e.g., Power BI, ServiceNow, SharePoint analytics).

Experience leveraging AI‑assisted tools (e.g., Copilot, ChatGPT) to enhance communications, analysis, or engagement.

Exposure to regulated, government, health, or enterprise environments.

Experience contributing to OCM practice maturity, tooling, or capability uplift initiatives.

Qualifications & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Communications, Business, Psychology, Organisational Development, Human Resources, or a related field.

2–5 years of relevant experience in Organisational Change Management, transformation, or related roles.

Prosci or equivalent change management certification (or progress toward certification) preferred.

Equivalent combination of education and experience will be considered.

About Us

At Rezdy, we’re builders, doers, and difference-makers, driven by a shared mission to reshape the tours, activities, and experiences industry. Alongside our sister brands, Checkfront and Regiondo, we power more than 20,000 businesses and support over $10B in bookings globally. Our technology helps operators thrive while delivering unforgettable moments to travelers around the world.

We work in an industry built on adventure, energy, and human connection, and that same spirit fuels how we show up every day. Spanning North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific, our teams are united by bold goals, a bias for action, and an unwavering commitment to delivering for our customers.

Our success starts with people. Our teams are the engine behind everything we create. We value self-starters who take ownership, embrace challenges, and raise the bar, for themselves and those around them. We believe in creating space to grow, take risks, and make a real impact, and we celebrate those who lead with curiosity, grit, and drive.

If you’re passionate about performance, hungry to learn, and ready to help shape the future of travel—this is your kind of place. Let’s build, grow, and win together.


About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Data Engineer with 5+ years of experience to join our data team. Our data platform powers analytics for both internal business users and external customers, and plays a key role in enabling data-driven decision making across the organization.

In this role, you will primarily focus on maintaining and enhancing our existing data platform, ensuring reliability, performance, and scalability. You will also work closely with internal stakeholders to design and implement new data models in our Enterprise Data Warehouse as new reporting and analytics requirements emerge.

The platform currently supports the data and analytics needs across Rezdy, Checkfront, and Regiondo, and provides reporting capabilities to both operational teams and customers.

Our modern data stack includes Google BigQuery, Fivetran, dbt, Metabase & GoodData. This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys working with mature data platforms, improving existing pipelines, and delivering high-quality data models that directly support business outcomes.

This is a one year fixed contract hybrid position based in Sydney, Australia. Candidates must have Australian permanent residency or citizenship to be considered.


What you will do

Maintain and support the enterprise data platform and existing data pipelines.

Ensure reliable data ingestion, transformation, and reporting across Rezdy, Checkfront, and Regiondo.

Monitor, troubleshoot, and resolve issues across the data stack to ensure data accuracy and availability.

Work closely with internal business users to understand their reporting and analytics requirements.

Design and implement new data models in BigQuery using dbt based on business needs.

Maintain and manage data ingestion pipelines.

Deliver clean, well-structured datasets that power reporting and dashboards in GoodData & Google sheets.

Optimize BigQuery queries and data models for performance and cost efficiency.

Ensure data quality, documentation, and governance across the data platform.

Contribute to improving data engineering standards, monitoring, and platform reliability.


What we are looking for

5+ years of experience in data engineering or analytics engineering roles.

Strong experience with SQL and data modeling in modern cloud data warehouses.

Hands-on experience with Google BigQuery.

Experience using dbt for transformation and data modeling.

Experience with data ingestion tools such as Fivetran or similar ELT platforms.

Experience supporting analytics platforms or BI tools.

Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts and dimensional modeling.

Experience maintaining and operating production data pipelines and platforms.

Ability to work with business stakeholders to translate requirements into scalable data models.

Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills.

Good documentation and communication skills.

About Us

Potentia Energy (previously Enel Green Power Australia) is a dynamic joint venture entity co-owned by two global energy leaders, Enel Green Power and INPEX, both committed to driving Australia’s energy transition and supporting the nation in achieving its net zero target. 

https://potentiaenergy.com.au/

With a proven track record and extensive expertise across the project lifecycle, Potentia Energy has established a strong presence in the Australian renewable energy sector. After acquiring a significant number of projects, our current operating portfolio currently comprises over 800MW of solar and wind capacity across Australia, with construction underway on a further approximate 200MW of capacity. This acquisition has doubled our current portfolio and has secured Potentia Energy a strong position in WA. Potentia Energy also has rights secured for a development pipeline of over 9GW across Australia and is committed to an ambitious growth agenda, targeting a significant increase to its installed capacity across wind, solar, storage and hybrid projects.

Guided by our core values – Collaboration, Authenticity, Responsibility and Excellence – we work closely with local communities, our partners and customer to contribute to a cleaner, more sustainable future.

 




The Role

Reporting to the Senior Wholesale and Commercial Manager, this role will be focused on optimising the bidding strategies of Potentia’s renewable and BESS generating assets and providing key analysis functions to support Potentia’s wholesale and retail businesses. We are looking for a proactive, motivated team player to provide crucial inputs into Potentia’s understanding of the market, wholesale risk management and renewable/battery asset operations.

This role will be key to understanding and managing Potentia’s physical and financial risks in the energy markets, protecting revenue and optimising operations.

 

Key Responsibilities

Manage bidding and dispatch service providers for Potentia Energy’s operational portfolio of solar, wind and BESS assets, including:
Being Potentia’s interface with service providers, managing the day to day relationships.
Developing execution strategies and performance reporting frameworks for monitoring and directing bidding and dispatch service providers.
Produce daily and monthly reporting (physical trading, derivative trading, financial and performance) with high attention to detail.
Provide regular analysis and reporting on energy market behaviour to support trading decisions and broader strategy, including generation and price trends and the regulatory environment.
Assist the wholesale team in the development and execution of hedging strategies.



Selection Criteria

A knowledge of Australian wholesale electricity markets (NEM and WEM) including physical and derivative trading fundamentals
Knowledge and experience of physical bidding, dispatch and Frequency Control Ancillary Services (FCAS)
Strong aptitude and experience in the visual presentation of data and analysis including slides and dashboards 
Intermediate knowledge of energy derivatives and power purchase agreements
Exceptional attention to detail
A positive attitude to operate in a fast paced and high performing team with accountabilities to global sponsors
Knowledge of relational databases (SQL) and/or the Energy One Trading system will be an advantage but not essential
Programming experience using high-level interpreted languages such as Python will be an advantage but not essential.
The right to live and work in Australia.

Our Benefits

Every individual is a valued team member at Potentia Energy, and we’re committed to supporting, growing, and helping each person achieve their full potential. Keeping up a positive work-life balance is a key to success for every team across our business.
Some of our benefits include:

Flexible working hours – we provide flexible arrangements that will suit you, including a mixture of time working in our office and from home, compressed work week with half day workday on Fridays. Because you are working from home, we also provide $50 internet reimbursements each month.
Health and wellbeing initiatives – including $100 wellness rebate each month, annual flu vaccinations and health checks, and a day off for your birthday each year. If you’re in the office, we have $35 lunch credits that you can use each week.
Learning and development opportunities – both on the job and via formal training including technical, role specific and leadership development programs.
Employee Assistance Program – confidential and independent counselling and advice service for either a personal or work-related problem if you or a member of your immediate family needs help or advice.

Diversity & Inclusion

Equal opportunity and inclusion are the pillars of our work. Throughout Potentia Energy, and in our head office in Sydney, many different languages, cultures, and stories coexist. Different ages, genders, cultural and ethnic backgrounds and skills complement each other and add value across our business.
We appreciate and value each person’s uniqueness. We encourage diversity of thought and the expression of ideas and opinions. We also make a commitment to foster equality of opportunity as part of creating a rewarding workplace for you. No kind of discrimination will be tolerated. All candidates and colleagues always receive the same treatment, based on their professional skills and abilities.
